commit.message:
fix(cmi): correct sample playback speed — was 11x too slow ("whale")

Root cause: CMI hardware pitch model reads wave_ram at ~1220 samples/sec
for middle C, designed for 128-sample page loops. Real Fairlight samples
at 14080 Hz would play at 0.087x speed — 13 seconds for a 1-second sample.

Fix: Added sample_mode flag — when a sample >256 bytes is loaded,
processSample() uses linear addressing with sample_inc = playRate/sampleRate
where playRate = 14080 * (freq/261.63). Hardware page-stepping mode preserved
for built-in waveforms.

Verified: 261 Hz output for MIDI 60 with 14080 Hz test sine (ratio 0.998x).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix GT Ultra row position: divide pattptr by 4 (byte offset → row index)

pattptr is a byte offset into pattern data (4 bytes/row: note, inst,
cmd, data). gt_get_current_row() was returning the raw byte offset,
causing the pattern editor to jump 4 positions per actual row advance.

Also handle the ENDPATT sentinel (0x7fffffff) by returning 0.

commit.message:
Smooth pattern editor scrolling for GT Ultra playback

Added FormatPlaybackState singleton — a lightweight format-agnostic
playback state that any format engine can write to. The
PatternEditorCanvas RAF loop reads it every frame (no React props in
the hot path) and computes smooth sub-pixel scroll offset using
time-based interpolation, identical to normal mode.

Changes:
- New: FormatPlaybackState.ts — tracks row, timestamp, rolling-average
  row duration for smooth interpolation
- GTUltra worklet: increase position update rate from 32 blocks (~93ms)
  to 2 blocks (~5.8ms) for responsive row tracking
- useGTUltraEngineInit: pipes position callbacks to FormatPlaybackState
- FT2Toolbar: sets FormatPlaybackState playing on play/stop
- PatternEditorCanvas: format mode RAF loop reads FormatPlaybackState
  directly (not React props) and computes smoothOffset per frame

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix GT Ultra pattern editor not following playback

useGTUltraFormatData was reading isPlaying/currentRow from
useTransportStore (the standard tracker engine transport), but GT Ultra
has its own WASM engine with its own playback state. The transport store
values were always false/0 during GT Ultra playback.

Now reads playing and playbackPos.row from useGTUltraStore, which is
correctly updated by the worklet's position callback every 32 frames.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix GT Ultra silent playback: initialize editorInfo in gt_play()

gt_play() didn't initialize editorInfo.maxSIDChannels, adparam, or
multiplier before starting the sequencer. When .sng files lack the
ID=0x1f extended info block, these stay at 0 (the C global default).

With maxSIDChannels=0, the playroutine() channel loop in gplay.c
skips ALL channels — no notes are sequenced, producing a brief thud
(initial SID gate) then silence.

Added the same safety defaults already used by gt_export_prg/sid:
  maxSIDChannels=3, adparam=0x0f00, multiplier=1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Claude/relaxed noether (#10)

* feat: scaffold full-stack Nextra app (Next.js + C++ Drogon)

Complete greenfield scaffold for a production-quality gamified web app
with AI chat integration, backed by a high-performance C++ Drogon API
compiled to a native binary.

Frontend (Next.js 16 + TypeScript):
- MUI with MD3 design tokens, dark/light mode via colorSchemes API
- Redux Toolkit + RTK Query + Redux Persist
- next-intl i18n (en/es) with proxy.ts locale routing
- Atomic design: 9 atoms, 8 molecules, 11+ organisms (<100 LOC each)
- 11 custom hooks (useAuth, useAiChat, useGamification, etc.)
- Full accessibility: data-testid, aria-*, tabIndex, keyboard shortcuts
- Constants in JSON, JSDoc on exports, strict TypeScript

Backend (C++ Drogon + Conan 2):
- 6 controllers (Auth, Users, Gamification, Notifications, Chat, Health)
- 7 services (Auth, Email/SMTP, Gamification, Notifications, AI, Migration, User)
- 3 filters (JWT auth, CORS, rate limiting)
- PostgreSQL with 8-table schema + SQL migrations + JSON seed data
- CLI11 entry point (serve, migrate, seed, create-admin subcommands)
- jwt-cpp, mailio, nlohmann/json, spdlog, fmt

Tooling (C++, zero Python/shell):
- CMake generator (inja + JSON + filesystem glob)
- Project manager CLI (build, test, docker, lint, fmt, generate, migrate, seed)
- clang-format + clang-tidy configs

DevOps:
- Multi-stage Dockerfiles for both services
- Docker Compose (dev + prod)
- CapRover captain-definition files
- GitHub Actions: CI, Docker/GHCR, release, CodeQL, deploy, dependabot
- CODEOWNERS, PR template, dependency review

Documentation:
- README, CLAUDE.md, agents.md, roadmap.md, plan.md
- docs/api.md, docs/deployment.md, docs/architecture.md

feat: Day 8 — LLM-generated LaTeX with 2-stage AI review

Replace template filler with intelligent LLM pipeline:

Stage 1 — LLM generates complete .tex from scratch:
- Follows 10 universal resume rules
- Experienced vs fresher detection
- Injects job requirements and improvement notes
- Proper section ordering (experience-first for experienced)

Stage 2 — LLM reviews its own output:
- Checks 20-item compliance checklist
- Fixes LaTeX syntax errors
- Verifies formatting rules
- Returns corrected .tex

Resume Rules enforced:
- One page only
- Action verb + metric on every bullet
- ATS-safe formatting (no fontspec, pdflatex compatible)
- Keywords from job description
- No contact info in body
- Technologies inline in projects

Providers: NVIDIA NIM (~1 min) or Ollama qwen3:14b (~1.5 min)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

hardcoded val===0, fixing Klystrack (emptyValue=0xFF). Use '---' for

commit.message:
Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.
